message:
refactor(storage,guard): single-request coalescer fast path + enumerate-based guard retry loop

Two parallel cleanups in the storage crate's commit coalescer and
the guard crate's git-retry loop. No behaviour changes in the
happy path; both read more cleanly and remove indexing boilerplate
that clippy had been flagging.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-storage/src/lib.rs
- coalescer_commit_batch: hoist the single-request fast path out
  of the secondary match arm and put it first. Previously the
  order was:
    1. `can_merge && requests.len() <= configured_batch_size` →
       merge path.
    2. `requests.len() == 1` → direct commit.
  which meant every single-request call evaluated `can_merge` and
  the merge-ceiling comparison even though those are irrelevant
  for a batch of one. New order:
    1. `requests.len() == 1` → direct commit.
    2. `can_merge && requests.len() <= configured_batch_size` →
       merge path.
  Behaviourally identical — just cheaper and easier to read. The
  single-request branch now commits directly via
  `coalescer_commit_with_retry(repo_root, &config,
  &requests[0].message, &requests[0].rel_paths)`, matching the
  shape the merge branch produces for its own call.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-guard/src/lib.rs
- guard_run_git_with_retry: replace the `for attempt in
  0..=BACKOFFS_MS.len()` index-based loop with an iterator that
  enumerates `(attempt, Some(base)) + (N, None)`:
    BACKOFFS_MS.into_iter()
        .map(Some)
        .chain(std::iter::once(None))
        .enumerate()
  The final `None` element carries the "no more backoffs left,
  break out of the loop" case so we don't need the separate
  `attempt >= BACKOFFS_MS.len()` check and don't index into the
  slice at all. Idiomatic Rust, slightly shorter.
- The inner jitter calc that reads `d.subsec_nanos() as u64` is
  now `u64::from(d.subsec_nanos())` per clippy's `cast_lossless`
  lint. Identical value, no narrowing cast.
- The `.map(|d| …).unwrap_or(0)` chain over the `SystemTime`
  Result collapses to `.map_or(0, |d| …)` — one fewer allocation
  closure, same semantics.
- Log spans unchanged; the tracing::warn!(
  target: "mcp_agent_mail::guard::segfault_retry", ...) calls
  still emit on both "retry attempted" and "retries exhausted"
  paths with the same `attempt =` tag so log consumers see no
  difference.
